root_text.an9.62:1.1: “Nava, bhikkhave, dhamme appahāya abhabbo arahattaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an9.62:1.3: Rāgaṁ, dosaṁ, mohaṁ, kodhaṁ, upanāhaṁ, makkhaṁ, paḷāsaṁ, issaṁ, macchariyaṁ—
root_text.an9.62:1.4: ime kho, bhikkhave, nava dhamme appahāya abhabbo arahattaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an9.62:2.1: Nava, bhikkhave, dhamme pahāya bhabbo arahattaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an9.62:2.3: Rāgaṁ, dosaṁ, mohaṁ, kodhaṁ, upanāhaṁ, makkhaṁ, paḷāsaṁ, issaṁ, macchariyaṁ—
root_text.an9.62:2.4: ime kho, bhikkhave, nava dhamme pahāya bhabbo arahattaṁ sacchikātun”ti.

translation_text.an9.62:1.1: “Mendicants, without giving up nine things you can’t realize perfection.
translation_text.an9.62:1.3: Greed, hate, delusion, anger, acrimony, disdain, contempt, jealousy, and stinginess.
translation_text.an9.62:1.4: Without giving up these nine things you can’t realize perfection.
translation_text.an9.62:2.1: After giving up nine things you can realize perfection.
translation_text.an9.62:2.3: Greed, hate, delusion, anger, acrimony, disdain, contempt, jealousy, and stinginess.
translation_text.an9.62:2.4: After giving up these nine things you can realize the fruit of perfection.”
